titleStmt.title: Capitula Proverbiorum in Paris, Bibliothèque Mazarine, MS 4

publicationStmt.p: Digital editions of summaries in Medieval Bibles

bibl.author: Donatien de Bruyne
bibl.title: Sommaires, divisions et rubriques de la Bible latine

bibl.editor: Monachi abbatiae pontificiae Sancti Hieronymi in Urbe
bibl.title: Biblia Sacra iuxta Latinam vulgatam versionem ad codicum fidem... Libri Salomonis id est Proverbia Ecclesiastes Canticum Canticorum ex interpretatione sancti Hieronymi cum praefationibus et variis capitulorum seriebus

tagsDecl.rendition: the digital reproduction is b/w, impossible to determine color

front.p:
The series of capitula published here is unknown to both de Bruyne and the Benedictine edition of the Vulgate. Since it can be argued to derive (see below) from the series called A in the Benedictine edition, we label it A(c)_dobips. Among the manuscripts recorded in the DOBiPS database, it appears only in P36_2 (Paris, Bibliothèque Mazarine, MS 4), the second volume of a complete Bible of French origin dating from the late 11th or early 12th century.
Some features of this series suggest that it may derive from a corrupted form of the series edited by the Benedictines under the siglum A, which comprises two slightly different versions (A(a) and A(b), corresponding to series A and Antipel in de Bruyne's classification).
First of all, the first three chapters are very similar, if not identical, to those in A(a) or A(b):
1 de parabolis Salomonis A(a), A(b) : parabole salomonis A(c) (see also below)
2 affectu patris adloquitur in uno omnes et in omnibus unum filium blanditiis peccatorum et hereticorum non adquiescere A(a) : affectu patris alloquitur filium blanditiis peccatorum non
adquiescere A(b) : affectu patris aloquitur blandiens filium A(c)
3 admonet affectu quo supra ne ambules cum impiis vel hereticis A(a) : non ambulandum cum
impiis A(b), A(c) Secondly, all the other chapters (4-58) correspond to the beginning of a given passage within the text of Proverbia; and almost all of these passages, in turn, coincide with the beginning of a chapter in manuscripts following the series A or (less often) Antipel, according to the data reported by de Bruyne from individual manuscripts representative of the various series (p. 478). See, e.g. (further details are given throughout the edited text):
4 sapientia foris prȩdicat A(c) = Prv 1.20 = ch. 4 in A
14 fili mi ausculta A(c) = Prv 4.20 = ch. 14 in A
28 ruina proximat malum A(c) = Prv 12.13 (propter peccata labiorum ruina proximat malo) = ch. 28
in A
38 qui inuenit mulierem A(c) = Prv 18.22 = ch. 38 in A It must be said, however, that the correspondence is not always perfect. For example, cap. 36 in A(c) reproduces the opening of Prv 17.17, while the A series places its chapter 36 at Prv 17.15; cap. 50 in A(c) coincides with Prv 26.4, while chap. 50 in A coincides with Prv 26.5. The end of the series presents the most problematic situation: cap. 57 in A(c) reproduces Prv 30.18, which corresponds to chap. 58 in A, thus leaving chap. 57 without a match; similarly, cap. 58 in A(c) reproduces Prv 31.10, that is chap. 59 in A, while chap. 60 is left without any correspondence. On the other hand, de Bruyne relied on a single witness that may have contained its own errors, and his data do not always match those of the Benedictine edition. For instance, according to the Benedictines, chap. 36 corresponds to Prv 17.17 as in A(c).
On the basis of these remarks, it can be suggested that behind A(c) lay a manuscript that followed series A(a) or A(b), but that, for some reason, no longer presented the complete table of chapters, but only the first three capitula, which in A(c) are almost identical to the "standard" ones. The remaining capitula, by contrast, had to be inferred from the chapter numbers found throughout the text of Proverbia; on this basis, the most straightforward option was adopted, namely the reproduction of each chapter's opening. This may also have led to the modification of the first three capitula – or only of the first two, if A(c) derives from A(b)/Antipel, whose third capitulum already coincides with that of A(c) (see above). Note that the first capitulum of A(c) also coincides with the opening of Prv 1.1.
The point in the text of Proverbia where P36_2 places the chapter number corresponding to each capitulum can be accessed either by clicking on the two-page icon next to each capitulum number, or by clicking on the "Passi paralleli" thumbnail (in the right-hand panel of the "Testo di lettura" screen).

bibl.title: Proverbia 1:1 parabole salomonis
Proverbia 1:7 timor domini
Proverbia 1:10 fili mi si te lactauerint
Proverbia 1:20 sapientia foris praedicat
Proverbia 1:23 convertimini ad correptionem meam
Proverbia 1:26 ego quoque in interitu vestro ridebo

item.title: Ego quoque in interitu uestro ridebo

bibl.title: Proverbia 2:1 fili mi si susceperis sermones meos
Proverbia 3:1 fili mi ne obliviscaris legis meae
Proverbia 3:5 habe fiduciam in domino
Proverbia 3:11 disciplinam domini fili mi
Proverbia 3:21 fili mi ne effluant haec ab oculis tuis
Proverbia 4:1 Audite filii disciplinam patris
Proverbia 4:10 audi fili mi et suscipe verba mea
Proverbia 4:20 fili mi ausculta sermones meos
Proverbia 5:1 fili mi adtende sapientiam meam
Proverbia 6:1 fili mi si spoponderis
Proverbia 6:16 sex sunt quae odit Dominus
Proverbia 6:20 conserua fili mi
Proverbia 6:27 numquid abscondere potest homo
Proverbia 7:1 fili mi custodi sermones meos
Proverbia 8:10 accipite disciplinam meam
Proverbia 8:22 dominus possedit me initium viarum
Proverbia 9:1 sapientia aedificavit sibi domum
Proverbia 9:10 principium sapientiae timor domini
Proverbia 10:1 filius sapiens laetificat patrem
Proverbia 11:1 statera dolosa abominatio apud dominum
Proverbia 11:24 alii dividunt propria et ditiores fiunt
Proverbia 12:13 propter peccata labiorum ruina proximat malo
Proverbia 13:3 qui custodit os suum custodit animam suam
Proverbia 14:1 sapiens mulier aedificavit domum suam
Proverbia 14:12 est via quae videtur homini iusta

item.title: Est uia quȩ uidetur homini iusta

bibl.title: Proverbia 14:34 iustitia elevat gentem
Proverbia 15:17 melius est vocare ad holera
Proverbia 15:26 abominatio domini cogitationes malae
Proverbia 16:28 homo perversus suscitat lites
Proverbia 17:17 omni tempore diligit qui amicus est
Proverbia 18:3 impius cum in profundum venerit
Proverbia 18:22 qui invenit mulierem invenit bonum
Proverbia 19:17 feneratur domino qui miseretur pauperis
Proverbia 20:9 quis potest dicere mundum est cor meum
Proverbia 20:23 abominatio est apud deum pondus
Proverbia 21:3 facere misericordiam et iudicium
Proverbia 21:24 superbus et arrogans vocatur indoctus
Proverbia 22:24 noli esse amicus homini iracundo
Proverbia 23:15 fili mi si sapiens fuerit animus tuus
Proverbia 24:11 erue eos qui ducuntur ad mortem
Proverbia 24:16 septies enim cadet iustus et resurget
Proverbia 24:30 per agrum hominis pigri transivi
Proverbia 25:17 subtrahe pedem tuum de domo proximi tui
Proverbia 26:4 ne respondeas stulto iuxta stultitiam suam
Proverbia 26:24 labiis suis intellegitur inimicus
Proverbia 27:5 melior est manifesta correptio
Proverbia 27:25 aperta sunt prata

item.title: Aperta sunt parata prata in Prv 27:25

bibl.title: Proverbia 28:10 qui decipit iustos in via mala
Proverbia 29:11 totum spiritum suum profert stultus
Proverbia 29:27 abominantur iusti virum impium
Proverbia 30:18 tria sunt difficilia mihi
Proverbia 31:10 mulierem fortem quis inveniet
